reason_for_request: 根据 unresolved cluster 请求 LLM 给出注释链接修补建议

request_prompt.system: 你是 FNM 注释修补助手（同时处理 endnote 和 footnote 两种 note_system）。只处理已经确认的 unresolved cluster，不要改 section、note zone、标题或原文。优先依据页面截图判断，不要被 OCR 坏掉的数字误导。如果截图已经足够清楚，就不要退回 needs_review。你只能输出 JSON 数组；每项 action 只能是 match、ignore_ref、synthesize_anchor、synthesize_note_item 或 needs_review。match 需要 note_item_id、anchor_id、confidence、reason；ignore_ref 需要 anchor_id、confidence、reason；synthesize_anchor 仅在正文里能找到一个独一无二的短语锚点、但该锚点没有对应的结构化 anchor 记录时使用，需要 note_item_id、anchor_phrase（从正文中原样抄写的 3~12 词唯一短语，不要自编）、confidence、reason；synthesize_note_item 仅在截图里能清楚看到同页注释文本、但 OCR / 结构化流程完全没产出 note item 时使用，需要 anchor_id、marker、note_text、confidence、reason；若某条 note 当前只是错误地绑到了 synthetic / 跨页锚点，而截图清楚显示它应改绑到当前显式锚点，也直接用 match，不要 needs_review。needs_review 需要 reason。
request_prompt.user:
/no_think
下面是一个 FNM unresolved cluster。请只返回 JSON 数组，不要解释。
本次只允许动作：ignore_ref、synthesize_note_item、needs_review。
若截图已经能明确判断坏掉的数字或同页注释，请直接输出可自动落地的动作，不要退回 needs_review。
若截图里清楚看到同页注释文本、但 OCR / 结构化数据里没有 note item，优先用 synthesize_note_item；marker 必须是截图上可见的数字，note_text 只抄注释正文，不要自编，不要补全看不清的部分。

cluster.chapter_title: Mad acts, mad speech, and mad people in Chinese medicine and law

unmatched_anchors.source_text: I will show that the Qing legal field reacted neither to a condition (“madness”) nor to a kind of person (“the mad”), but first to individual acts of killing that officials attributed to insanity. “Killing because of madness” was a category of legal practice, not a label imposed upon a pre-existing reality. Through legislative and judicial practices, this penal denomination generated an official view of madness that differed from medical conceptions of kuang. $ ^{6} $

page_contexts.ocr_excerpt: according to specific legal procedures, categories, and concerns. They have also tended to take madness as a ready-made target for the new laws, or to posit a uniform and unchanging referent: “lunatics” (Bünger 1950), “the mentally ill” (Nakamura 1973d and Hao 2002), “insane persons” (MacCormack 1992), and “insanity” and “the insane” (Ng 1990). Martha Li Chiu hinted at more complexity when she claimed that Qing law responded to “a particular kind of human crisis defined very early in classical medical texts under the label of kuang 狂” (Chiu 1981: 75), but she still suggested that the legal institution responded to a pre-existing object—albeit a culturally determined one—outside of itself. I will show that the Qing legal field reacted neither to a condition (“madness”) nor to a kind of person (“the mad”), but first to individual acts of killing that officials attributed to insanity. “Killing because of madness” was a category of legal practice, not a label imposed upon a pre-existing reality. Through legislative and judicial practices, this penal denomination generated an official view of madness that differed from medical conceptions of kuang. $ ^{6} $ Until recently, there had been no serious historical work on whatever may be called madness in Chinese medicine. The many studies that tried to identify precursors to modern psychiatry did not take the original Chinese categories ...
